quizQuestion:
Mi hermano es {___} yo.
(My brother is taller than me.)
sentenceTranslation: My brother is taller than me.
grammarPointLabel: Comparative of superiority: más + adj + que
explanation: más alto que — comparative of superiority. Structure: más + [adjective] + que. 'que' introduces the second element of comparison.

quizQuestion:
María es {___} su hermano.
(María is smarter than her brother.)
sentenceTranslation: María is smarter than her brother.
grammarPointLabel: Comparative of superiority: más + adj + que
explanation: más inteligente que — comparative: más + inteligente + que. Most Spanish adjectives use más/menos for comparison (no -er/-est suffix like English).

quizQuestion:
Este hotel es {___} el otro.
(This hotel is less expensive than the other one.)
sentenceTranslation: This hotel is less expensive than the other one.
grammarPointLabel: Comparative of inferiority: menos + adj + que
explanation: menos caro que — comparative of inferiority. Structure: menos + [adjective] + que.

quizQuestion:
Pedro es {___} tú.
(Pedro is as intelligent as you.)
sentenceTranslation: Pedro is as intelligent as you.
grammarPointLabel: Comparative of equality: tan + adj + como
explanation: tan inteligente como — comparative of equality. Structure: tan + [adjective] + como. 'tan' precedes the adjective; 'como' introduces the comparison.

quizQuestion:
Este examen no es {___} parece.
(This exam is not as difficult as it seems.)
sentenceTranslation: This exam is not as difficult as it seems.
grammarPointLabel: Comparative of equality: tan + adj + como (negated)
explanation: tan difícil como — equality comparison. The negative form 'no es tan X como Y' is very common in spoken Spanish.

quizQuestion:
Mi hermano es {___} que yo.
(My brother is older than me.)
sentenceTranslation: My brother is older than me.
explanation: mayor — irregular comparative of grande/viejo when referring to age or size. 'más viejo' also acceptable but 'mayor' is preferred. Contrast: mayor (older) vs el mayor (the oldest).

quizQuestion:
Ana es {___} que su hermano.
(Ana is younger than her brother.)
sentenceTranslation: Ana is younger than her brother.
explanation: menor — irregular comparative of pequeño/joven when referring to age. Contrast with mayor (older).

quizQuestion:
Este libro es {___} que ese.
(This book is better than that one.)
sentenceTranslation: This book is better than that one.
explanation: mejor — irregular comparative of bueno. Never say 'más bueno' for quality — use 'mejor'. Exception: 'más buena persona' (better person) is OK for character.

quizQuestion:
El tiempo hoy está {___} que ayer.
(The weather today is worse than yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: The weather today is worse than yesterday.
explanation: peor — irregular comparative of malo. Never say 'más malo' — use 'peor'. Exception: 'más malo' can mean 'meaner/nastier' colloquially.

quizQuestion:
Carlos es {___} de la clase.
(Carlos is the most intelligent in the class.)
sentenceTranslation: Carlos is the most intelligent in the class.
explanation: el más inteligente — superlative. Structure: el/la/los/las + más/menos + adjective + de [group]. 'De' introduces the group of comparison.

quizQuestion:
Es {___} restaurante de la ciudad.
(It is the best restaurant in the city.)
sentenceTranslation: It is the best restaurant in the city.
explanation: el mejor — irregular superlative of bueno. 'el mejor' = the best (masculine). Superlatives use the article + irregular form: el mejor, la mejor, el mayor, la mayor.

quizQuestion:
Fue {___} día de mi vida.
(It was the worst day of my life.)
sentenceTranslation: It was the worst day of my life.
explanation: el peor — irregular superlative of malo. 'el peor' = the worst (masculine). 'el peor día de mi vida' is a very common phrase.
